heading: Competitive grants to States and Indian tribes for the development of loan programs to facilitate the widespread adoption of certified EHR technology

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The National Coordinator may award competitive grants to eligible entities for the establishment of programs for loans to health care providers to conduct the activities described in subsection (e).
submits to the National Coordinator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the National Coordinator may require;

submits to the National Coordinator a strategic plan in accordance with subsection (d) and provides to the National Coordinator assurances that the entity will update such plan annually in accordance with such subsection;
provides assurances to the National Coordinator that the entity will establish a Loan Fund in accordance with subsection (c);

children.label: i
children.text: the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (or his or her designee), in the case of an entity participating in the Medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] or the Medicaid program under title XIX of such Act [ et seq.]; or42 U.S.C. 139542 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary in the case of other entities;

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrate to the satisfaction of the Secretary (through criteria established by the Secretary) that any certified EHR technology purchased, improved, or otherwise financially supported under a loan under this section is used to exchange health information in a manner that, in accordance with law and standards (as adopted under ) applicable to the exchange of information, improves the quality of health care, such as promoting care coordination; andsection 300jj–14 of this title1So in original. The word “and” probably should appear at end of subpar. (D).1

children.label: C
children.text: comply with such other requirements as the entity or the Secretary may require;

children.label: D
children.text: include a plan on how health care providers involved intend to maintain and support the certified EHR technology over time;

children.label: E
children.text: include a plan on how the health care providers involved intend to maintain and support the certified EHR technology that would be purchased with such loan, including the type of resources expected to be involved and any such other information as the State or Indian Tribe, respectively, may require; and
agrees to provide matching funds in accordance with subsection (h).

children.heading: Establishment of fund
children.label: c Establishment of fund
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b)(3), an eligible entity shall establish a certified EHR technology loan fund (referred to in this subsection as a “Loan Fund”) and comply with the other requirements contained in this section. A grant to an eligible entity under this section shall be deposited in the Loan Fund established by the eligible entity. No funds authorized by other provisions of this subchapter to be used for other purposes specified in this subchapter shall be deposited in any Loan Fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b)(2), a strategic plan of an eligible entity under this subsection shall identify the intended uses of amounts available to the Loan Fund of such entity.

children.label: A
children.text: A list of the projects to be assisted through the Loan Fund during such year.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the criteria and methods established for the distribution of funds from the Loan Fund during the year.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the financial status of the Loan Fund as of the date of submission of the plan.

children.label: D
children.text: The short-term and long-term goals of the Loan Fund.
facilitate the purchase of certified EHR technology;

enhance the utilization of certified EHR technology (which may include costs associated with upgrading health information technology so that it meets criteria necessary to be a certified EHR technology);
train personnel in the use of such technology; or
improve the secure electronic exchange of health information.

children.label: A
children.text: The interest rate for each loan shall not exceed the market interest rate.

children.label: B
children.text: The principal and interest payments on each loan shall commence not later than 1 year after the date the loan was awarded, and each loan shall be fully amortized not later than 10 years after the date of the loan.

children.label: C
children.text: The Loan Fund shall be credited with all payments of principal and interest on each loan awarded from the Loan Fund.

To guarantee, or purchase insurance for, a local obligation (all of the proceeds of which finance a project eligible for assistance under this subsection) if the guarantee or purchase would improve credit market access or reduce the interest rate applicable to the obligation involved.

As a source of revenue or security for the payment of principal and interest on revenue or general obligation bonds issued by the eligible entity if the proceeds of the sale of the bonds will be deposited into the Loan Fund.
To earn interest on the amounts deposited into the Loan Fund.
To make reimbursements described in subsection (g)(4)(A).

children.heading: Combined financial administration
children.label: 1 Combined financial administration
children.text: An eligible entity may (as a convenience and to avoid unnecessary administrative costs) combine, in accordance with applicable State law, the financial administration of a Loan Fund established under this subsection with the financial administration of any other revolving fund established by the entity if otherwise not prohibited by the law under which the Loan Fund was established.

children.heading: Cost of administering fund
children.label: 2 Cost of administering fund
children.text: Each eligible entity may annually use not to exceed 4 percent of the funds provided to the entity under a grant under this section to pay the reasonable costs of the administration of the programs under this section, including the recovery of reasonable costs expended to establish a Loan Fund which are incurred after .February 17, 2009

children.label: A
children.text: provisions to ensure that each eligible entity commits and expends funds allotted to the entity under this section as efficiently as possible in accordance with this subchapter and applicable State laws; and

children.label: B
children.text: guidance to prevent waste, fraud, and abuse.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A Loan Fund established under this section may accept contributions from private sector entities, except that such entities may not specify the recipient or recipients of any loan issued under this subsection. An eligible entity may agree to reimburse a private sector entity for any contribution made under this subparagraph, except that the amount of such reimbursement may not be greater than the principal amount of the contribution made.

children.heading: Availability of information
children.label: B Availability of information
children.text: An eligible entity shall make publicly available the identity of, and amount contributed by, any private sector entity under subparagraph (A) and may issue letters of commendation or make other awards (that have no financial value) to any such entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The National Coordinator may not make a grant under subsection (a) to an eligible entity unless the entity agrees to make available (directly or through donations from public or private entities) non-Federal contributions in cash to the costs of carrying out the activities for which the grant is awarded in an amount equal to not less than $1 for each $5 of Federal funds provided under the grant.

children.heading: Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.label: 2 Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.text: In determining the amount of non-Federal contributions that an eligible entity has provided pursuant to subparagraph (A), the National Coordinator may not include any amounts provided to the entity by the Federal Government.2So in original. Probably means “paragraph (1),”.2

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: i Effective date
children.text: The Secretary may not make an award under this section prior to .January 1, 2010
